chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presented with atypical chest pain.
history: The patient was admitted into hospital for chest pain and an abnormal flow in the pulmonary artery observed on transthoracic echocardiography in the emergency department. Further investigations revealed a coronary-to-pulmonary fistula connecting the left anterior descending artery to the pulmonary artery trunk. The patient had a peptic ulcer, which was successfully treated.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Transthoracic echocardiography: abnormal flow in the pulmonary artery; Myocardial perfusion scan: normal perfusion in the left anterior descending artery territory; Upper digestive endoscopy: peptic ulcer.
